summary: leo-cron ships binding.gyp with GYP command-expansion executing shell at install time
details: binding.gyp at line 6 contains GYP command-expansion syntax (<!(...) / <!@(...) / <@(...)) inside the targets/sources fields. npm implicitly invokes node-gyp rebuild whenever a package contains a binding.gyp, and GYP evaluates command-expansion expressions as shell during the configure step — so the embedded command runs automatically on every `npm install` of this package, even though package.json declares no install/postinstall script. This is functionally equivalent to a lifecycle hook and executes attacker-chosen code on the installer's machine. The package has no apparent legitimate need for a native addon build via binding.gyp, indicating the file's only purpose is to fire the embedded shell command at install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
